title: herniaria hirsuta subsp. riphaea
intro: Herniaria hirsuta subsp. riphaea, also known as Himalayan sweethearts, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its hairy stems and white, star-shaped flowers. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its demulcent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe digestive issues and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Himalayan sweethearts include aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and other conditions. Himalayan sweethearts can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and may also be used in tincture or capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
